claimText: The Standing Deposit Facility (SDF) is a monetary policy instrument used by the RBI to absorb liquidity from commercial banks without the need for providing collateral.
evidenceExcerpt: The Standing Deposit Facility (SDF) is a monetary policy instrument used by the RBI to absorb liquidity from commercial banks without the need for providing collateral.
examUse: Statement-based question to test understanding of SDF's core function and its distinction from other tools.
distractorHooks: SDF is used to inject liquidity.
SDF is not a monetary policy instrument.

claimText: The Standing Deposit Facility (SDF) has replaced the Fixed Rate Reverse Repo as the floor of the Liquidity Adjustment Facility (LAF) corridor.
evidenceExcerpt: It has replaced the Fixed Rate Reverse Repo as the floor of the Liquidity Adjustment Facility (LAF) corridor.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question on the structure of the LAF corridor.
distractorHooks: SDF is the ceiling of the LAF corridor.
SDF replaced the Marginal Standing Facility (MSF).
SDF operates alongside the Fixed Rate Reverse Repo as the floor.

claimText: The SDF is an overnight facility for liquidity absorption, though the RBI retains the flexibility to extend its duration.
evidenceExcerpt: The SDF is an overnight facility, available daily, with the RBI having the flexibility to extend its duration.
examUse: Statement-based question testing the operational characteristics of the SDF.
distractorHooks: SDF is strictly an overnight facility with no flexibility in duration.
SDF is a long-term liquidity absorption tool.
The duration of SDF is determined by commercial banks.

claimText: The Standing Deposit Facility is available for use every day, including on holidays and weekends.
evidenceExcerpt: Available every day, including holidays and weekends.
examUse: Statement-based question focusing on the operational availability of monetary policy instruments.
distractorHooks: SDF is only available on banking weekdays.
SDF is available only during specific auction timings.
SDF availability is limited to month-end.

claimText: Any entity eligible for the Liquidity Adjustment Facility (LAF) is permitted to use the Standing Deposit Facility.
evidenceExcerpt: Any entity eligible for LAF can utilize this facility.
examUse: Statement-based question about the eligibility criteria for participating in RBI's liquidity facilities.
distractorHooks: Only scheduled commercial banks can use the SDF.
SDF is open to all financial institutions, including those not eligible for LAF.
Eligibility for SDF is more restrictive than for LAF.
